items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Italian Smart Living 2026 has arrived in Addis Ababa
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 08 LUG - Italian Smart Living 2026 has arrived in Addis Ababa. The business and design initiative is promoted by the Italian Trade Agency (ICE) and the National Confederation of Crafts and SMEs, with the support of the Ethiopian Embassy. The event is designed to strengthen commercial and institutional ties between Italy and Ethiopia, showcasing the excellence of “Made in Italy” solutions in the fields of design, construction, and smart living. Hosted at the Ethiopian Science Museum, the initiative serves as a platform connecting Italian manufacturers, designers, and solution pro
items.titleIt: Italian Smart Living 2026 has arrived in Addis Ababa

items.title: Somalia: Somaliland President Pardons 609 Prisoners Nationwide
items.description: [Horn Diplomat] Hargeisa -- Somaliland President Abdirahman Mohamed Abdullahi (Irro) on Tuesday granted a general presidential pardon to 609 prisoners convicted of minor offences, exercising his constitutional authority to extend clemency to eligible inmates.
items.titleIt: Somalia: Somaliland President Pardons 609 Prisoners Nationwide

items.title: Kenya: 'We Fear for Our Lives' - Kisumu Activists Explain Absence From Saba Saba Protests
items.description: [Capital FM] Kisumu -- For the first time in recent memory, Kisumu remained calm during the annual Saba Saba commemorations, with businesses operating normally, public transport running uninterrupted and traffic flowing freely across the lakeside city as organisers of planned protests cited security concerns for staying away from the streets.
items.titleIt: Kenya: 'We Fear for Our Lives' - Kisumu Activists Explain Absence From Saba Saba Protests

items.title: Russian FM Lavrov begins Africa tour with talks in Ethiopia
items.description: Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov began his African tour with a visit to Ethiopia on Tuesday, where he met with Mahamoud Ali Youssouf, chairperson of the African Union Commission.
items.titleIt: Russian FM Lavrov begins Africa tour with talks in Ethiopia

items.title: In Ukraine, Italy is investing in the future and in the health of mothers and children
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 08 LUG - Every child born in Ukraine represents a promise for the future. Ensuring they receive quality care, from the very first days of life—even in the context of war—means contributing not only to saving lives but also to laying the foundations for the country’s recovery. This is the vision that inspires Italy’s commitment to supporting the Ukrainian healthcare system. At the international conference *“Neonatal Care in War-Torn Ukraine”*, organized by CUAMM – Doctors with Africa in Odessa, Italy reaffirmed that maternal and child health is a strategic priority in its coopera
items.titleIt: In Ukraine, Italy is investing in the future and in the health of mothers and children

items.title: Govt parties present bills to boost repatriations, tighten rules on citizenship
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 8 - Premier Giorgia Meloni's right-wing Brothers of Italy (FdI) party and Deputy Premier and Transport Minister Matteo Salvini's right-wing League party have presented bills in parliament to make it easier for Italy to send migrants back to their countries of origin and tighten the rules on Italian citizenship. Under a bill presented by FdI, all non-EU nationals given prison terms of more than one year will be deported to their home countries via bilateral agreements to be negotiated with those countries. FdI House whip Galeazzo Bignami said the crackdown was made possible b
items.titleIt: Govt parties present bills to boost repatriations, tighten rules on citizenship

items.title: Ambassador Di Gianfrancesco Meets with Azerbaijan’s Minister of Digital Development
items.description: (ANSA) - ROMA, 08 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Baku, Luca Di Gianfrancesco, met with Azerbaijan’s Minister of Digital Development and Transport, Rashad Nabiyev. Together, they discussed the potential for further collaboration between companies in the two countries, including within the framework of projects to develop regional connectivity. Read article...
items.titleIt: Ambassador Di Gianfrancesco Meets with Azerbaijan’s Minister of Digital Development

items.title: Conflict pushes South Sudan's healthcare system to breaking point
items.description: South Sudan's worsening conflict is placing enormous pressure on the country's healthcare system, with the International Committee of the Red Cross reporting a 50 percent rise in medical evacuations during the first half of 2026.
items.titleIt: Conflict pushes South Sudan's healthcare system to breaking point

items.title: Sudan: UN Condemns RSF Escalation Around El Obeid As Tasees Backs Ceasefire
items.description: [Dabanga] El Obeid / El Fasher / Geneva / Chad -- The UN Human Rights Council has unanimously condemned the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) for escalating violence in and around El Obeid, warning that the North Kordofan capital faces a growing risk of mass atrocities, while the RSF-aligned Sudan Founding Alliance (Tasees) called for an immediate ceasefire and renewed political negotiations.
items.titleIt: Sudan: UN Condemns RSF Escalation Around El Obeid As Tasees Backs Ceasefire

items.title: Woman describes 'ordeal' after ECHR reverses Italy 'sexist verdict'
items.description: (ANSA) - ROME, JUL 6 - A woman who was awarded damages after the European Court of Human Rights quashed what it called a "sexist and stereotypical" verdict" acquitting her sexually abusive husband because "it's normal for a man to overcome a woman's resistance" before sex told an Italian daily Monday that she had been through an "ordeal" since she reported the domestic rape in 2021. "Justice has finally been done. It doesn't seem real after so long. I went through an ordeal," Audrey Ubeda, now 42, said in an interview with la Repubblica, after the ECHR ruled in her favour, ordering Italy to co
items.titleIt: Woman describes 'ordeal' after ECHR reverses Italy 'sexist verdict'
